https://nust.edu.pk/admissions/fee-struc: UG fee structure (2025-26): Admission processing fee PKR 35,000 (non-refundable), security deposit PKR 10,000 (refundable), tuition per semester PKR 197,050 for Engineering/Computing/Natural/Applied Sciences/HND and PKR 275,400 for Architecture/Social Sciences/Business. Miscellaneous charges PKR 5,000 per semester. International UG: admission fee USD 600, tuition USD 5,400 per annum, security USD 250, health USD 120 per annum.
https://nust.edu.pk/admissions/NSHS/fee-: MBBS fee structure (Session 2025-26): National students total programme cost PKR 12,249,828; foreign students total USD 101,160. Includes refundable security (PKR 25,000 national / USD 400 foreign). Annual tuition and administrative charges vary by year.
https://nust.edu.pk/admissions/alliedpro: BSHND fee structure: National students admission fee PKR 35,000 (non-refundable), security PKR 10,000 (refundable), tuition PKR 197,050 per semester, miscellaneous PKR 5,000 per semester. International students: admission USD 600, security USD 250, tuition USD 5,400 per annum, health USD 120 per annum.

BS Human Nutrition and Dietetics (BSHND) admission criteria: minimum 60% in SSC (science) and 60% in HSSC pre-medical (Biology, Chemistry, Physics) or equivalent. Accepts NET-Applied Sciences / NUMS MDCAT / National MDCAT with minimum 50% marks, or ACT with minimum required scores.
BSHND key dates (as published): online registration started 15 Dec 2025, closed 15 Jan 2026 (with fee submission), tentative merit/selection in Jan 2026, tentative class commencement in Feb 2026.
https://nust.edu.pk/admissions/NSHS/how-: MBBS (NSHS) application route is through centralized MBBS admissions portal and NUMS process. Candidates apply online using the official MBBS/BDS admission platform and follow the published procedure document for open merit and foreign seats.
https://nust.edu.pk/admissions/NSHS/meri: MBBS merit generation (open merit): NUMS MDCAT 50%, HSSC/A-level/equivalent 40%, SSC/O-level/equivalent 10%. For foreign seats: entry test component 50% (NUMS MDCAT/SAT-II/MCAT/UCAT), HSSC/A-level 40%, SSC/O-level 10%.
https://nust.edu.pk/admissions/undergrad: ACT/SAT basis admissions: national and international categories can apply as per policy. Typical baseline includes minimum 60% in SSC and HSSC/equivalent, valid ACT/SAT scores as required by programme, IBCC equivalence for foreign qualifications, and separate merit lists for ACT/SAT route.

UG eligibility baseline: minimum 60% aggregate in SSC and HSSC/equivalent (with IBCC equivalence for foreign qualifications). Programme-specific subject combinations apply; some tracks may require additional mathematics deficiency/condensed courses as per policy.

Academic qualification mapping by programme: eligibility depends on HSSC/equivalent subject background (e.g., Pre-Engineering, Pre-Medical, ICS, A-level/foreign). Different backgrounds are mapped to eligible Engineering, Computing, Natural Sciences, Architecture/Design, and Social/Business/Law programmes.
DAE eligibility: minimum 60% in Matric and DAE, admission on open merit. DAE backgrounds are mapped to specific engineering/computing programmes as approved by policy.

NET subjects/weightings vary by NET type. NET-Engineering: Maths 50%, Physics 30%, English 20%. NET-Applied Sciences: Biology 50%, Chemistry 30%, English 20%. NET-Business/Social Sciences: Quantitative Maths 50%, English 50%. NET-Architecture: Design Aptitude 50%, Maths 30%, English 20%. NET-Natural Sciences: Maths 50%, English 50%.
https://campuslife.nust.edu.pk/facility-: Housing and dining: NUST provides hostel accommodation with category-wise monthly charges and refundable security. Facilities include furnished rooms, mess, laundry, internet, and student support services. Separate rates exist for national, international, and MBBS categories.
https://ugadmissions.nust.edu.pk/result/: NET/UG admissions result portal entry point for checking published results and related updates.
http://nshsadmission.nust.edu.pk/hnd/: BS HND admissions portal for merit/selection updates and related notices.

NET subjects and weightings by test category (Engineering, Applied Sciences, Business/Social Sciences, Architecture, Natural Sciences).
